author: Ari Koslow
title: Luke Kornet (illness) available Saturday
desc: Luke Kornet is available for Game 5 of the NBA Finals on Saturday against the Knicks.
impact: Kornet is good to go after being listed as questionable. He'll remain in a very minimal role off the bench.

author: Ari Koslow
title: Mitchell Robinson (finger) listed available for Game 5 Saturday
desc: Mitchell Robinson is available for Saturday's Game 5 against the Spurs.
impact: Robinson is back on the injury report, but he is good to go. He will remain in his reserve role behind Karl-Anthony Towns.

author: Ari Koslow
title: Victor Wembanyama: No flagrant foul upgrade by NBA
desc: There was no flagrant upgrade on the uncalled foul of Victor Wembanyama to Jalen Brunson on Monday night in Game 3.
impact: Wembanyama will stay at two flagrant points in postseason. The Knicks lead the series 2-1 going into Wednesday.

author: Ari Koslow
title: Jalen Brunson (knee/ankle) off injury report Friday
desc: Jalen Brunson is off the injury report for Game 2 of the NBA Finals on Friday against the Spurs.
impact: Brunson suffered knee and ankle injuries in Wednesday's Game 1 win. He went back to the locker room briefly early before later returning and playing the rest of the game. He is good to go heading into Game 2.

author: Ari Koslow
title: De'Aaron Fox (ankle) off injury report Wednesday
desc: De'Aaron Fox is off the injury report for Game 1 of the NBA Finals against the Knicks on Wednesday.
impact: Fox played through his ankle injury to end the Western Conference Finals. Spurs coach Mitch Johnson said Fox's sore right ankle is "trending toward 100 percent."

author: Ari Koslow
title: Cason Wallace to start in Game 7 Saturday
desc: Cason Wallace will enter the starting lineup for the Thunder in Game 7 on Saturday against the Spurs.
impact: Wallace will return to the starting lineup with Jared McCain returning to his reserve role.

author: Ari Koslow
title: Jalen Williams (hamstring) did not suffer setback
desc: Thunder coach Mark Daigneault says Jalen Williams didn't suffer any setback in Game 6.
impact: Daigneault said Williams did not undergo the normal return-to-play procedure leading into his return. If Oklahoma City advances to the NBA Finals, Williams will continue his rehab.

author: Ari Koslow
title: Kyrie Irving (knee) says he is 'close' to 100 percent in his recovery
desc: Kyrie Irving said on a Twitch stream Friday night that he is  "close to being over at 100%" in his recovery.
impact: Irving is over 14 months removed from the ACL surgery he underwent. It appears he will be fully healthy for Mavericks training camp that begins in late September.

author: Diego Sandoval
title: Ajay Mitchell (calf) ruled out for Game 7 Saturday
desc: Ajay Mitchell has been ruled out for Saturday's Game 7 against the Spurs with a calf injury.
impact: Mitchell will miss his fourth consecutive game on Saturday with a calf injury. The Thunder will continue to lean on Jared McCain, Cason Wallace and Alex Caruso as scoring options with Mitchell sidelined.
